name: Rice Pudding with Brandy Currants
recipeInstructions:
1. This is a lighthearted version of riz a l'imperatrice -- "rice in the style of the empress" - which is thought to have been inspired by Empress Eugenie, the Spanish wife of Napoleon.
2. In a small saucepan, bring together 2 tablespoons dried currants and brandy to boil.
3. Remove the mixture from heat. In another small saucepan, combine 1-1/2 cups water, rice, and salt. Bring to boil, stirring occasionally.
4. Reduce heat to low. Cook uncovered until almost all water is absorbed and rice is very soft, stirring occasionally, about 20 minutes.
5. Place 2 cups milk in a heavy medium saucepan; sprinkle gelatin over milk. Let stand for 10 minutes to soften.
6. Heat the milk mixture over low heat until just warm, stirring until gelatin dissolves. Add currant mixture, rice, sugar, orange peel, and cinnamon.
7. Cook over low heat until the mixture thickens slightly, stirring occasionally, about 12 minutes; do not boil.
8. Whisk egg and vanilla in a medium bowl to blend. Mix half of the hot rice mixture into the egg.
9. Return the egg mixture to the saucepan and stir over very low heat for 3 minutes; do not boil.
10. Remove the saucepan from the ice water. Stir the pudding occasionally until cool. Spoon into four 3/4-cup custard cups. Refrigerate until set, at least 8 hours.
PER SERVING: calories, 230; fat, 4 g; sodium, 113 mg; cholesterol, 63 mg
Source: Bon Appetit (May 1994)
